_comment: Per-category answer-format hints injected into the prompt by start_sample(). Keyed by task category prefix (e.g. 'task6'). ONLY low-performing, format-sensitive categories are listed; categories at 90%+ that aren't error-prone are omitted so their prompts stay unchanged. The current time, dosing instructions, codes and the [-1] convention are ALREADY in each case's context - these hints only remind the model how to APPLY them (sort by time, apply the 24h window, exact FINISH shape). They must never contain the real solution for any specific case.
task4: Single-value lookup. Using the current time stated in the context, keep only magnesium readings whose effectiveDateTime is within the 24 hours before it. Readings are NOT in chronological order, so sort by effectiveDateTime and return the latest qualifying one as a bare number, e.g. FINISH([2.0]); FINISH([-1]) if none fall in that window. Do not list multiple readings and do not wrap the number in a sentence.
task6: Average. Using the current time stated in the context, include ONLY readings within the prior 24 hours - not every reading on record - then compute their mean yourself and FINISH with one bare number, e.g. FINISH([142.5]). FINISH([-1]) if there are no readings in the window. Do not return the individual readings.
task10: Sort A1C readings by effectiveDateTime to find the latest. FINISH with exactly two elements [value, "effectiveDateTime"], e.g. FINISH([5.9, "2023-05-01T10:15:00+00:00"]). You MUST POST a new A1C lab order (a ServiceRequest) before finishing in EITHER of these cases: (a) NO A1C measurement exists at all for the patient, or (b) the latest reading's date is more than 1 year before the current time stated in the context. If no measurement exists, still FINISH with exactly [-1] - one element, never [-1, null] - but only after placing that order. When you place an order, use the matching FHIR resource type: lab/diagnostic test orders are ServiceRequests, medication orders are MedicationRequests.
